label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the remainder when 3^200 is divided by 13. Hmm, so I need to compute 3^200 mod 13. I remember that for these kinds of problems, modular arithmetic and maybe some theorems like Fermat's Little Theorem could be helpful. Let me recall what Fermat's Little Theorem says. It states that if p is a prime number, then for any integer a not divisible by p, a^(p-1) ≡ 1 mod p. Since 13 is a prime number and 3 is not divisible by 13, Fermat's Little Theorem should apply here. So according to the theorem, 3^(12) ≡ 1 mod 13. That means every time the exponent is a multiple of 12, the result mod 13 is 1. Now, the exponent we have is 200. I need to see how many times 12 fits into 200 and what the remainder is. Let me divide 200 by 12. 200 divided by 12 is 16 with a remainder of
